name: capesolo
documentation:
Python GUI to run capemon in standalone VM. Provides a subset of CAPE processing and results.

* Create a Windows 10 VM that's suitable for running malware.
  * Use the CAPEv2 guest guide for configuration details.
  * https://capev2.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation/guest/index.html
* Install Python in VM, tested on 64-bit Python versions 3.11 and 3.12, and add Python to path.
* Download and install Microsoft Visual C++ Redistributable.
  * https://aka.ms/vs/17/release/vc_redist.x86.exe
  * https://aka.ms/vs/17/release/vc_redist.x64.exe
* Install CAPEsolo.
  * pip install CAPEsolo
* Snapshot your VM.

Quick Start 
* Open an administrator command window.
* Type capesolo <return> to run.

Alternatively, create a shortcut to CAPEsolo.exe, 
which will be in the Scripts subdirectory of same location as your python.exe file. 
* Under Advanced, check 'Run as administrator'
* An icon file is available in the CAPEsolo install folder under site-packages.

Analysis results are found in C:\Users\Public\CAPEsolo\analysis.
* Can be configured in python-path\site-packages\CAPEsolo\cfg.ini

Revert the VM after each analysis.

documentation:
Determine which unserializers (or converters) have to be used in
order to parse the various arguments for this function call. Maintains
whether the current bson is 32-bit or 64-bit.

Interprets .bson logs from the monitor.
The monitor provides us with "info" messages that explain how the function
arguments will come through later on. This class remembers these info
mappings and then transforms the api call messages accordingly.
Other message types typically get passed through after renaming the
keys slightly.

functions.name: CAPEsolo.capelib.url_validate.url
documentation:
Return whether or not given value is a valid URL.
If the value is valid URL this function returns `True`, otherwise
{class}`~validators.utils.ValidationFailure`.
This validator is based on the wonderful [URL validator of dperini].
.. \_URL validator of dperini:

> <https://gist.github.com/dperini/729294>

Examples::
: ```pycon
  >>> url('http://foobar.dk')
  True
  >>> url('ftp://foobar.dk')
  True
  >>> url('http://10.0.0.1')
  True
  >>> url('http://foobar.d')
  ValidationFailure(func=url, ...)
  >>> url('http://10.0.0.1', public=True)
  ValidationFailure(func=url, ...)
  ```

:::{versionadded} 0.2
:::

:::{versionchanged} 0.10.2 Added support for various exotic URLs and fixed various false positives.
:::

:::{versionchanged} 0.10.3 Added ``public`` parameter.
:::

:::{versionchanged} 0.11.0 Made the regular expression this function uses case insensitive.
:::

:::{versionchanged} 0.11.3 Added support for URLs containing localhost
:::

```
param value

URL address string to validate

param public

(default=False) Set True to only allow a public IP address
```

Runs signtool.exe and parses the output.

For this to work, the Microsoft tool signtool.exe will need to be placed
into the windows/analyzer/bin/ directory. signtool.exe can be downloaded
from Microsoft as part of the SDK. (which is also usually packaged with
Visual Studio)

TODO:
Currently the only way to properly update root certificate cab files is
via Windows Update which is disabled in many Cuckoo rigs. This means that
we will not be able to error out on revoked certificates. Need to find a
good work around for this. Ideally make it an option so we don't force
log unnecessary update network traffic.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.lib.common.abstracts.Package.configure
documentation:
Do package-specific configuration.

Analysis packages can implement this method to perform pre-analysis
configuration in the execution environment. This method will be called
after the auxiliary modules are started but before the package start
method is called.

See the "configure_from_data" method for an alternative approach to
package-specific configuration that lets configuration be treated as
runtime data separate from the analysis package.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.lib.common.abstracts.Package.configure_from_data
documentation:
Do private package-specific configuration.

Analysis packages can implement this method to perform pre-analysis
configuration based on runtime data contained in "data/packages/<package_name>".

This method raises:
 - ImportError when any exception occurs during import
 - AttributeError if the module configure function is invalid.
 - ModuleNotFoundError if the module does not support configuration from data

methods.name: CAPEsolo.modules.auxiliary.amsi.EventConsumer._run
documentation:
Because ProcessTrace() blocks, this function is used to spin off new threads.

```
param trace_handle

The handle for the trace consumer that we want to begin processing.

param end_capture

A callback function which determines what should be done with the results.

return

Does not return a value.
```

methods.name: CAPEsolo.modules.auxiliary.amsi.EventConsumer._getEventInformation
documentation:
Initially we are handed an EVENT_RECORD structure. While this structure technically contains
all of the information necessary, TdhGetEventInformation parses the structure and simplifies it
so we can more effectively parse and handle the various fields.

```
param record

The EventRecord structure for the event we are parsing

return

Returns a pointer to a TRACE_EVENT_INFO structure or None on error.
```

methods.name: CAPEsolo.modules.auxiliary.amsi.EventConsumer._getMapInfo
documentation:
When parsing a field in the event property structure, there may be a mapping between a given
name and the structure it represents. If it exists, we retrieve that mapping here.

Because this may legitimately return a NULL value we return a tuple containing the success or
failure status as well as either None (NULL) or an EVENT_MAP_INFO pointer.

```
param record

The EventRecord structure for the event we are parsing

param info

The TraceEventInfo structure for the event we are parsing

param event_property

The EVENT_PROPERTY_INFO structure for the TopLevelProperty of the event we are parsing

return

A tuple of the map_info structure and boolean indicating whether we succeeded or not
```

methods.name: CAPEsolo.modules.auxiliary.amsi.EventConsumer._getPropertyLength
documentation:
Each property encountered when parsing the top level property has an associated length. If the
length is available, retrieve it here. In some cases, the length is 0. This can signify that
we are dealing with a variable length field such as a structure, an IPV6 data, or a string.

```
param record

The EventRecord structure for the event we are parsing

param info

The TraceEventInfo structure for the event we are parsing

param event_property

The EVENT_PROPERTY_INFO structure for the TopLevelProperty of the event we are parsing

return

Returns the length of the property as a c_ulong() or None on error
```

methods.name: CAPEsolo.modules.auxiliary.amsi.EventConsumer._unpackSimpleType
documentation:
This method handles dumping all simple types of data (i.e., non-struct types).

```
param record

The EventRecord structure for the event we are parsing

param info

The TraceEventInfo structure for the event we are parsing

param event_property

The EVENT_PROPERTY_INFO structure for the TopLevelProperty of the event we are parsing

return

Returns a key-value pair as a dictionary. If we fail, the dictionary is {}
```

methods.name: CAPEsolo.modules.auxiliary.amsi.EventConsumer._unpackComplexType
documentation:
A complex type (e.g., a structure with sub-properties) can only contain simple types. Loop over all
sub-properties and dump the property name and value.

```
param record

The EventRecord structure for the event we are parsing

param info

The TraceEventInfo structure for the event we are parsing

param event_property

The EVENT_PROPERTY_INFO structure for the TopLevelProperty of the event we are parsing

return

methods.name: CAPEsolo.modules.auxiliary.amsi.EventConsumer._processEvent
documentation:
This is a callback function that fires whenever an event needs handling. It iterates through the structure to
parse the properties of each event. If a user defined callback is specified it then passes the parsed data to
it.

```
param record

The EventRecord structure for the event we are parsing

return

Nothing
```

Wraps all interactions with Event Tracing for Windows (ETW) event consumers. This includes
starting and stopping the consumer. Additionally, each consumer begins processing events in
a separate thread and uses a callback to process any events it receives in this thread -- those
methods are implemented here as well.

N.B. If using this class, do not call start() and stop() directly. Only use through via ctxmgr

documentation:
Create and run Log Server.
@return: operation status.

Cuckoo Log Server.

This Log Server receives the BSON-encoded logs from cuckoomon loaded in an individual process
and forwards them on to the resultserver on the process' behalf, avoiding the need for winsock
in cuckoomon and escaping some deadlock issues that can arise from the use of winsock APIs at
crucial points in process execution (like the final termination) and also allowing us to again
use synchronous logging without side-effects.

functions.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.pids_from_image_names
documentation:
Get PIDs for processes whose image name ends with one of the given suffixes.

Matches are not sensitive to casing; both the suffixes and process
image names are normalized prior to comparison.

functions.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.in_protected_path
documentation: Checks if a path is protected.

functions.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.add_protected_path
documentation: Adds a pathname to the protected list

functions.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.upload_files
documentation: Create a copy of the given file path.

classes.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.Analyzer

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.Analyzer.prepare
documentation: Prepare env for analysis.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.Analyzer.stop
documentation: Allow an auxiliary module to stop the analysis.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.Analyzer.complete
documentation: End analysis.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.Analyzer.run
documentation:
Run analysis.
@return: operation status.

CAPE Windows Analyzer.

This class handles the initialization and execution of the analysis
procedure, including handling of the pipe server, the auxiliary modules and
the analysis packages.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.Files.is_protected_filename
documentation: Return whether or not to inject into a process with this name.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.Files.add_pid
documentation: Track a process identifier for this file.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.Files.add_file
documentation: Add filepath to the list of files and track the pid.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.Files.dump_file
documentation: Dump a file to the host.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.Files.delete_file
documentation: A file is about to be removed and thus should be dumped right away.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.Files.move_file
documentation: A file will be moved - track this change.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.Files.dump_files
documentation: Dump all pending files.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.ProcessList.add_pid
documentation:
Add a process identifier to the process list.
Track determines whether the analyzer should be monitoring this
process, i.e., whether Cuckoo should wait for this process to finish.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.ProcessList.add_pids
documentation: Add one or more process identifiers to the process list.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.ProcessList.has_pid
documentation: Return whether this process identifier being tracked.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.ProcessList.remove_pid
documentation: Remove a process identifier from being tracked.

classes.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.CommandPipeHandler

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.CommandPipeHandler._handle_debug
documentation: Debug message from the monitor.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.CommandPipeHandler._handle_info
documentation: Regular message from the monitor.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.CommandPipeHandler._handle_warning
documentation: Warning message from the monitor.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.CommandPipeHandler._handle_critical
documentation: Critical message from the monitor.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.CommandPipeHandler._handle_loaded
documentation: The monitor has loaded into a particular process.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.CommandPipeHandler._handle_getpids
documentation:
Return the process identifiers of the agent and its parent
process.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.CommandPipeHandler._handle_kterminate
documentation:
Handle terminate notification.
Remove pid from process list because we received a notification
from kernel land

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.CommandPipeHandler._handle_kprocess
documentation:
Handle process notification.
Same than below but we don't want to inject any DLLs because
it's a kernel analysis

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.CommandPipeHandler._handle_ksubvert
documentation:
Remove pids from the list.
If a new driver has been loaded, we stop the analysis.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.CommandPipeHandler._handle_service
documentation:
Start a service and monitor it.
Handle case of a service being started by a monitored process.
Switch the service type to own process behind its back so we
can monitor the service more easily with less noise.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.CommandPipeHandler._handle_shutdown
documentation:
Handle attempted shutdowns/restarts.
Flush logs for all monitored processes. Additional handling can be added later.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.CommandPipeHandler._handle_kill
documentation:
Handle case of malware terminating a process.
Notify the target ahead of time so that it can flush its log buffer.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.CommandPipeHandler._inject_process
documentation: Helper function for injecting the monitor into a process.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.CommandPipeHandler._handle_process
documentation: Request for injection into a process.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.CommandPipeHandler._handle_process2
documentation: Request for injection into a process using APC.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.CommandPipeHandler._handle_file_new
documentation: Notification of a new dropped file.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.CommandPipeHandler._handle_file_cape
documentation: Notification of a new dropped file.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.CommandPipeHandler._handle_file_del
documentation:
Notification of a file being removed (if it exists) - we have to
dump it before it's being removed.
In case of FILE_DEL, the client is trying to notify an ongoing
deletion of an existing file, therefore we need to dump it
straight away.

methods.name: CAPEsolo.analyzer.CommandPipeHandler._handle_file_move
documentation: A file is being moved - track these changes.

Pipe Handler.
This class handles the notifications received through the Pipe Server and
decides what to do with them.
